the_signal.headline: Agent identity and intent remain half-solved in production systems
the_signal.body: n8n's analysis of 75+ agent capabilities across Google, Claude, and Gumloop reveals that reliable agent execution still lacks standards for identity management and intent verification. This is a GTM blocker: your AI sales agents can't scale without knowing who they are or what they're supposed to do, and the tooling hasn't caught up to the problem.

tool_drop.name: Gemini 3.6 Flash Family
tool_drop.what: Google's latest multimodal model with faster inference and lower latency targeting real-time agent workloads.
tool_drop.why: If your revenue agents hit latency walls during customer interactions, this is worth benchmarking against Claude to see if you can cut response time without sacrificing reasoning quality.

thread_of_day.title: Claude Code vs n8n: when to code vs orchestrate your automation
thread_of_day.takeaway: Claude Code solves single-task automation fast; n8n scales when you need multi-step workflows, visibility, and infrastructure control — pick based on whether you need speed or reliability.

quote.text: Agent identity, reliable execution, and intent are only halfway solved — after reviewing hundreds of tech docs, 75 capabilities are still missing standards across platforms.

whats_moving.context: n8n published a deep evaluation guide, signaling that GTM teams building on untrusted infrastructure need composable compliance workflows, not black-box solutions.
New n8n template combines RAG + threat intel + historical incidents; your revenue ops team should be thinking about automating deal-breaking technical support escalations.
Google and competitors still lack consensus on how agents handle identity and intent — this fragmentation means your agent architecture decisions today will lock you in for 12+ months.

act_on_this: Test whether your current agent setup can answer 'who am I?' and 'what am I trying to do?' in production without manual intervention — if not, you have a scaling ceiling at 10-50 concurrent customer interactions.

numbers.stat: 75 agent capabilities still lack standardization across major platforms
numbers.context: This fragmentation means you're building custom glue code instead of deploying proven patterns — your technical debt in agent orchestration is growing faster than your revenue automation.
